message:
fix(membrane): harden fingerprint, page oracle, and runtime math subsystems

Fingerprint subsystem:
- Widen allocation size field from u32 to u64 (FINGERPRINT_SIZE 16→20 bytes)
  to support allocations larger than 4 GiB without silent truncation. All
  callers in arena.rs and ptr_validator.rs updated to pass u64 sizes.
- Replace fixed compile-time SipHash keys with runtime-initialized random
  keys seeded via getrandom(2) on Linux, falling back to ASLR-derived
  entropy. This prevents an attacker who reads the binary from predicting
  fingerprint hashes and crafting forged allocation headers.
- Extend SipHash message from 2 words to 3 words (addr, size, generation)
  now that size is u64 and no longer packed with generation.

Arena subsystem:
- Bump minimum alignment from 16 to 32 bytes to satisfy the new
  FINGERPRINT_SIZE of 20 bytes (alignment must be >= header size and a
  power of 2).

Page oracle:
- Widen per-page refcount from AtomicU8 to AtomicU32 to prevent premature
  saturation at 255 concurrent allocations per page. Programs with dense
  small allocations (e.g., many 32-byte nodes) could previously saturate
  the counter and lose accurate page liveness tracking.

Pointer validator:
- Fix TLS cache hit path: recompute remaining bytes relative to the query
  address rather than using the stale full-allocation remaining. A cached
  entry for base=0x1000 size=256 queried at addr=0x1100 would previously
  report remaining=256 instead of the correct 156 bytes, allowing
  out-of-bounds writes to go undetected.
- Mark interior pointers that fall outside [user_base, user_base+user_size)
  as Invalid rather than returning the cached state.

Heal policy:
- Handle dst_remaining == Some(0) explicitly by returning ClampSize{0}
  instead of falling through to the TruncateWithNull path which would
  underflow (saturating_sub(1) on 0 = 0, but semantically wrong).

Runtime math:
- alpha_investing: use saturating_mul to prevent overflow when wealth is
  large and SPEND_FRACTION_MILLI multiplication exceeds u64 range.
- conformal: return f64::INFINITY when the quantile rank exceeds the
  sample count instead of clamping to the last element, which would
  undercount the prediction interval width.
- control (primal-dual): clamp lambda accumulators to [-2M, +2M] via
  fetch_update to prevent unbounded drift, and use u128 intermediate
  for adverse_ppm to prevent overflow when bad * 1_000_000 exceeds u64.

TLS cache:
- Replace per-entry invalidation loop with entries.fill(EMPTY) for
  correctness (ensures all fields including generation are zeroed) and
  marginal performance improvement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
